title: Puffed Russian Potato Pancakes
description: Savory, aerated and nostalgic Russian potato pancakes infused with sweet Mars bar and served in scallop-shell vessels.
instructions: Preheat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and lightly oil it with olive oil.
In a large bowl, combine grated potatoes, potato starch, baking powder, salt, pepper, and dill, mixing until well incorporated.
Beat the egg and stir it into the potato mixture until a cohesive batter forms.
Add cold water gradually, whisking until the batter reaches a pourable yet thick consistency.
Gently fold in melted Mars bar, ensuring small chocolate pockets remain within the batter.
Spoon the batter into individual scallop-shell-shaped molds or fill rinsed scallop shells with batter, smoothing the top with a spatula.
Place the scallop shells on the preheated skillet, cover partially with a lid, and cook for 4 minutes until the edges begin to puff and brown.
Flip the scallop shells carefully and cook the other side for an additional 3 minutes, allowing the pancakes to puff fully.
Remove from heat and let them sit for 1 minute before serving inside the scallop shells to retain shape.
Optionally, drizzle leftover melted Mars bar over the pancakes and sprinkle fresh dill for garnish.
